Bizarre Lineage Tier List (March 2026)

The definitive Bizarre Lineage tier list ranking every Stand for the current meta. Data aggregated from the top 100 PvP leaderboard and verified through in-game testing by our team.

Choosing the right Stand is the single most important decision in Bizarre Lineage. This Bizarre Lineage tier list breaks down all 17 Stands into four clear tiers — S+, S, A, and B — based on their damage output, crowd control, mobility, combo potential, and performance across both PvP arenas and PvE boss raids.

Whether you are deciding which Stand to keep after using a Stand Arrow, or planning your next Prestige build, this Bizarre Lineage tier list gives you a data-driven answer. For detailed stats and movesets, visit each Stand's page in our Bizarre Lineage stands database.

S+

S+ Tier — Best Bizarre Lineage Stands

Overwhelmingly powerful. These Bizarre Lineage Stands dominate both PvP and PvE. Absolute must-haves for competitive play.

The World: High Voltage

Mythical
S+
DMG 10
SUS 5
PvP 10
Best: Boxing
S

S Tier — Meta Bizarre Lineage Stands

Exceptionally strong across almost all matchups. Highly recommended Bizarre Lineage Stands if S+ options are not available.

A

A Tier — Viable Bizarre Lineage Stands

Solid performers in the Bizarre Lineage tier list. Requires more mechanical skill to beat S-tier Stands, but perfectly viable for competitive and casual play.

Bizarre Lineage Tier List FAQ

How often is the Bizarre Lineage tier list updated?

We update this Bizarre Lineage tier list after every major game patch, balance change, or community-driven meta shift. Rankings are based on PvP leaderboard data and testing by experienced players.

What makes a Stand S+ tier in the Bizarre Lineage tier list?

S+ Stands in the Bizarre Lineage tier list deliver exceptional damage output, excellent crowd control, high mobility, and strong performance in both PvP arenas and PvE raids. They can solo carry matches consistently.

Should I reroll my Stand based on this Bizarre Lineage tier list?

If you have a B-tier Stand and enough Stand Arrows, rerolling is recommended. However, player skill matters — an experienced user with an A-tier Stand can beat a beginner with an S+ Stand. Use the tier list as a guideline, not a hard rule.

Does the Bizarre Lineage tier list change after updates?

Yes. Game balance patches frequently adjust Stand damage values, cooldowns, and passives. After each update we re-test every Stand and adjust positions on the Bizarre Lineage tier list accordingly.
